title: OverHarness Agent Definition
description: JSON Schema to validate OverHarness .agent.yaml files. Supports orchestrator agents (like Sheldon), specialist agents (like Penny, Leonard, Howard, Amy), brownfield agents (like Raj), and utility agents (like Bernadette).

agent.description: Agent metadata: identity, version, and inspiration.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for the agent (e.g., sheldon, penny, howard).

title.description: Agent title/role (e.g., GPS Orchestrator, Requirements Analyst).

inspiration.description: Inspiration reference for the persona (e.g., character name from a show).

model_role.description: Maps this agent to a role in overharness.yaml > model_profiles. Determines which AI model is used for this agent based on the active profile.

persona.description: Agent persona: role, identity, communication style, and guiding principles.

role.description: Functional role of the agent within the framework.

identity.description: Description of the agent's identity, personality, and behavior.

style.description: Agent's communication style — how it presents information and interacts.

catchphrase.description: Agent's signature catchphrase, used in appropriate moments.

principles.description: Principles that guide the agent's behavior and decisions.

phases.description: Phases in which this agent operates. Use 'all' for agents that operate in every phase (e.g., orchestrators).

language_protocol.description: Language configuration for agent responses. Can be a string (inline rule) or an object with structured fields.

response_language_source.description: Where to read the user's preferred response language (e.g., 'overharness.yaml > user_preferences.response_language').

rule.description: Rule describing how the agent should handle language.

menu.description: Available commands for the agent. Specialist agents use 'workflow' to reference a workflow path; orchestrator agents use 'action' to reference an internal behavior.

trigger.description: Command keyword that activates this menu item.

workflow.description: Path to the associated workflow (used by specialist agents).

action.description: Internal action identifier (used by orchestrator agents like Sheldon).

description.description: Human-readable description of what this command does.

activation.description: Ordered sequence of steps the agent performs when activated.

step.description: Short title of the activation step.

detail.description: Detailed instructions for this activation step.

first_run_behavior.description: Behavior for the agent's first run (e.g., language detection, project type detection). Used by Sheldon.

behaviors.description: Agent behaviors keyed by workflow/command name. Each behavior has a description and instructions.

description.description: Short description of what this behavior does.

instructions.description: Detailed instructions for executing this behavior.

verification_protocol.description: Verification protocol used by quality review agents (e.g., Amy's 3-level goal-backward verification).

atomic_commits.description: Atomic commit protocol for developer agents (e.g., Howard). Defines commit format and workflow.

deviation_rules.description: Rules for handling unexpected situations during implementation. Defines auto-fix vs. must-stop scenarios.

path.description: Path where the artifact will be saved.

template.description: Path to the template used to generate the artifact.

consumes.description: Artifacts that this agent consumes from other agents. Can be an array of objects (structured) or an array of strings (simple paths).

from.description: Agent ID that produces this artifact.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this artifact provides.
